---
title: "Home"
---

# Hi, I'm Aamir

Welcome to my personal website for **AAI1001 Data Engineering and Visualization**.

I am currently studying at the **Singapore Institute of Technology (SIT)**, where I am learning more about programming, data engineering, visualisation, and how digital tools can be used to communicate ideas clearly.

This website was created using **Quarto**, **RStudio**, **Git**, and **GitHub Pages**.

---

## A Small Photo Gallery

These are some photos I took at **Bird Paradise**. I chose to include them because they represent my interest in observing details, colours, and visual composition — something that also connects well with data visualisation.

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="33%"}
![Bird Paradise photo 1](images/bird1.jpg)
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
![Bird Paradise photo 2](images/bird2.jpg)
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
![Bird Paradise photo 3](images/bird3.jpg)
:::

:::

---

## About Me

I enjoy working with technology and learning how things are built, whether it is through programming, design, or hands-on projects.

Some areas I am currently exploring include:

- **R programming** for data analysis
- **Quarto** for creating documents and websites
- **Git and GitHub** for version control
- **Data visualisation** to present information clearly
- **Object-oriented programming** through Java-based projects

Outside of school, I am also interested in motorcycles, cars, photography, and tech-related projects.

---

## What I Am Working On

So far, my academic work has involved different types of projects, including:

- building a reusable **abstract game engine** using Java and LibGDX
- learning how to manage code using **GitHub**
- creating reports and documentation for technical projects
- exploring data tools through this new module
- practising how to present information through visual and written formats

This website will act as a small portfolio and learning space as I continue through the trimester.

---

## Why This Website Matters

Creating this website helps me understand how technical content can be presented online in a clean and accessible way.

It also gives me a place to document:

1. what I learn in this module,
2. projects I work on,
3. reflections from labs,
4. and examples of data visualisation work.

> This website is still a work in progress, but it represents the beginning of my learning journey in data engineering and visualisation.
